title: diplazium paripinnatum
intro: Diplazium paripinnatum, also known as the two-pinnate or paired-leaved leather fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Diplazium paripinnatum include its ability to relieve fever, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or tea, and it can also be dried and used in the form of capsules or tablets.
